hero_caption: Drift-detect → reconciliation cycle: a console-injected out-of-band change surfaced on the nightly plan, turned into a PR, reviewed, and reverted to the hardened baseline.

narration.caption: Baseline — terraform plan against the applied state shows no diff. 87/92 CIS controls passing. Zero public IPv4 on compute.
Drift injected via GCP Console: `gcloud compute firewall-rules create lab-allow-all-tcp --allow=tcp` — simulating an unauthorized out-of-band change.
Scheduled CI job runs `terraform plan`. Detects the rogue firewall rule as a resource to be destroyed (not in state).
CI auto-opens a PR with the drift delta. tfsec gate fails the PR because the injected rule is public-ingress-CRITICAL — confirming the architecture's defensive logic.
Operator approves the revert PR. CI applies. Terraform destroys the out-of-band firewall rule.
Post-apply verification: `gcloud compute firewall-rules list` shows only the terraform-managed rules. tfsec scan returns to 87/92 passing. State object in GCS shows one new version.

verification.criterion: Zero public IPv4 on compute tier after reconciliation
tfsec scan returns to 0 HIGH/CRITICAL findings
verification.method: tfsec --config-file .tfsec/config.yml --format=json terraform/ | jq '[.results[] | select(.severity=="HIGH" or .severity=="CRITICAL")] | length'

verification.criterion: No IAM service account keys were created during the incident window
verification.observed: Empty result — 0 SA key creation events.

verification.criterion: IAP tunnel sessions during reconciliation are attributable to the operator who approved the PR
verification.method: BigQuery scheduled query over the audit log sink: SELECT principalEmail, resource.labels.instance_id, timestamp FROM audit WHERE methodName LIKE '%IAP%' AND timestamp BETWEEN '<start>' AND '<end>'
verification.observed: 1 session logged: principalEmail=richard.mussell@yahoo.com (approving operator), instance_id matches the PR target.

verification.criterion: Terraform state object in GCS received exactly one new version
verification.observed: Pre-apply: 14 versions. Post-apply: 15 versions. Delta: +1 — matches expected state mutation.

prereqs: A GCP project with billing enabled and the following APIs: compute, iam, iamcredentials, secretmanager, logging, iap
An account with roles/owner on the project (for initial bootstrap only; CI uses reduced roles)
env_vars: GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=  # leave empty — uses Application Default Credentials
steps: # 4. (Optional) Inject drift to see detection in action
# 5. Re-run plan — expect the drift to surface
terraform plan  # will show the firewall-rule as planned-for-deletion
gcloud compute firewall-rules list | grep lab-test-drift  # should be empty

output_snippets.label: terraform plan — drift detection output (excerpt)
output_snippets.output:
$ terraform plan

Refreshing state...

Terraform detected the following changes made outside of Terraform:

  # google_compute_firewall.lab_allow_all_tcp has been created outside of Terraform
    + resource "google_compute_firewall" "lab_allow_all_tcp" {
      + allow              = [{ ports = [], protocol = "tcp" }]
      + creation_timestamp = "2026-04-15T03:22:14.871-07:00"
      + direction          = "INGRESS"
      + id                 = "projects/landing-zone-core/global/firewalls/lab-allow-all-tcp"
      + name               = "lab-allow-all-tcp"
      + network            = "projects/landing-zone-core/global/networks/lz-lab-vpc"
      + source_ranges      = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
    }

Unless you have made equivalent changes to your configuration, the next plan
or apply will propose removing these resources.

Plan: 0 to add, 0 to change, 1 to destroy.

output_snippets.label: tfsec scan output on the drift-injected state (PR gate)
output_snippets.output:
$ tfsec --config-file .tfsec/config.yml terraform/

Result #1 CRITICAL Firewall rule allows ingress from 0.0.0.0/0.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  terraform/drift.tfstate:17
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     14 |     direction     = "INGRESS"
     15 |     priority      = 1000
     16 |     name          = "lab-allow-all-tcp"
  >  17 |     source_ranges = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
     18 |     allow { protocol = "tcp" }
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    ID           google-compute-no-public-ingress
    Impact       Compute instance reachable from anywhere
    Resolution   Scope source_ranges to an authorized CIDR
    Severity     CRITICAL (override: project policy)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

timings
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  disk i/o           54.8ms
  parsing            22.1ms
  adaptation         3.9ms
  checks             81.2ms
  total              162ms

counts
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  severity     CRITICAL: 1
  total        1

output_snippets.label: Post-reconcile firewall-rules list (gcloud)
output_snippets.output:
$ gcloud compute firewall-rules list --project=landing-zone-core --format='table(name,direction,sourceRanges)'
NAME                        DIRECTION  SOURCE_RANGES
lz-lab-iap-ssh              INGRESS   ['35.235.240.0/20']
lz-lab-internal-all         INGRESS   ['10.0.0.0/8']
lz-lab-egress-cloud-apis    EGRESS    N/A

# lab-allow-all-tcp is gone. All remaining rules are terraform-managed and CIS-compliant.

not_demonstrated: Multi-region failover — lab is single-region, no cross-region state replication exercised.
Destroy path — `terraform destroy` is exercised in Terratest but not in this walkthrough. A production destroy flow would also need to delete the state bucket + WIF pool + IAM bindings manually.
Load testing — the landing zone was validated with 3 instances per tier; behavior at 300+ instances is not characterized.
Break-glass state rollback to a specific GCS version is documented in the runbook but not performed in this demo.
Cost optimization under sustained workload — IAP per-byte cost and Cloud NAT egress charges were measured at lab scale ($0.50/mo); production economics unknown.
